Current validation compares the exact requirement namespace to SPEC-QUIC-RFC9000 and rejects valid extra section grouping segments such as S...

notes: Advisory title-shape drift: 623 final titles in RFC 9000 include approved normative keywords and read like clauses rather than short descriptive labels. This is a `REQ-TPL-0029` SHOULD-level issue, not an identifier invalidity or clause-body blocker.
Assembly-map accounting is consistent in the current working tree: the only live one-to-many draft lineage is `REQ-RFC9000-S8-0007`, and that split is explicit in `9000.assembly-map.json`.
No merged duplicate currently loses source provenance in `9000.assembly-map.json`.
The repaired step-2 coverage artifact in `step2_out/9000/10.coverage.json` now accounts for `REQ-RFC9000-S103-0022`.

validator_policy_mismatches.message: Current validation compares the exact requirement namespace to SPEC-QUIC-RFC9002 and rejects valid extra section grouping segments such as S...

validator_policy_mismatches.issue: All 1736 QUIC validation errors are emitted as REQ-CLAUSE namespace-alignment failures because the validator requires an exact namespace match and does not allow extra section grouping segments after the specification context.
validator_policy_mismatches.conclusion: The section-scoped IDs satisfy SPEC-ID and artifact-id-policy rules; the blocker is validator policy mismatch, not imported-content invalidity.

repo_state_issues.issue: Running the repository validator on the canonical spec-trace suite yields 8 REQ-CLAUSE errors and 0 discovered requirements because the helper does not recognize the suite's linked REQ headings.

expected_non_blockers.issue: REQ-DOWNSTREAM warnings are expected because this QUIC import run did not create ARC artifacts, WI artifacts, or VER artifacts.
REQ-COVERAGE warnings are expected for the same reason and do not block this import audit.
